name: Dot product of two integer vectors

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_dot/dot.py — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T (test case count). Each case: line A is N (>= 0). Line B is N space-separated integers (a) — empty line if N=0. Line C is N space-separated integers (b). For each case, output the integer dot product sum(a[i]*b[i]) on its own line. Use a 64-bit accumulator (values can exceed 2^32). Pure Python; no numpy.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_dot; python3 dot.py < input.txt > out.txt; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_dot/dot.go (package main). Reads from stdin: line 1 is T (test case count). Each case: line A is N (>= 0). Line B is N space-separated integers (a) — empty line if N=0. Line C is N space-separated integers (b). For each case, output the integer dot product sum(a[i]*b[i]) on its own line. Use a 64-bit accumulator (values can exceed 2^32). Compile with `go build -o dot dot.go`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_dot; go build -o dot dot.go && ./dot < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_dot/dot.c — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T (test case count). Each case: line A is N (>= 0). Line B is N space-separated integers (a) — empty line if N=0. Line C is N space-separated integers (b). For each case, output the integer dot product sum(a[i]*b[i]) on its own line. Use a 64-bit accumulator (values can exceed 2^32). Compile with `gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o dot dot.c -lm`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_dot; gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o dot dot.c -lm && ./dot < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_dot/dot.cpp — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T (test case count). Each case: line A is N (>= 0). Line B is N space-separated integers (a) — empty line if N=0. Line C is N space-separated integers (b). For each case, output the integer dot product sum(a[i]*b[i]) on its own line. Use a 64-bit accumulator (values can exceed 2^32). Compile with `g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o dot dot.cpp`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_dot; g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o dot dot.cpp && ./dot < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_dot/dot.rs — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T (test case count). Each case: line A is N (>= 0). Line B is N space-separated integers (a) — empty line if N=0. Line C is N space-separated integers (b). For each case, output the integer dot product sum(a[i]*b[i]) on its own line. Use a 64-bit accumulator (values can exceed 2^32). Idiomatic Rust 2021+; compile with `rustc -O dot.rs -o dot`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_dot; rustc -O dot.rs -o dot 2>/dev/null && ./dot < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_dot/dot.zig — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T (test case count). Each case: line A is N (>= 0). Line B is N space-separated integers (a) — empty line if N=0. Line C is N space-separated integers (b). For each case, output the integer dot product sum(a[i]*b[i]) on its own line. Use a 64-bit accumulator (values can exceed 2^32). Use Zig 0.16+ idioms. Entrypoint: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`. For stdio, the rich API (`takeDelimiter`, `appendRemainingUnlimited`, `print`, `writeAll`, `flush`) lives on the `std.Io.Reader`/`Writer` *interface*, NOT on the concrete `std.Io.File.Reader`/`Writer`. Reach the interface via `&fr.interface`. Canonical setup: `var in_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined; var fr = std.Io.File.stdin().reader(init.io, &in_buf); const r = &fr.interface;` and the symmetric writer. Then `try r.takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns `?[]u8`, null at EOF) for line-by-line, or `try r.appendRemainingUnlimited(init.arena.allocator(), &list)` to slurp. Always `try w.flush()` before return — output is buffered. Strict-default footguns (errors, not warnings, in 0.16): rename unused params to `_`, discard unused locals with `_ = x;`, prefer `const` over `var` when not mutated. Use `std.Io` (capital I), not `std.io`. Compile with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast dot.zig`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_dot; zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast dot.zig && ./dot < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
